title: podocarpus polystachyus subsp. thevetiifolius
intro: Podocarpus polystachyus subsp. thevetiifolius, also known as the Thevetia Podocarpus, is a plant species belonging to the family Podocarpaceae, characterized by its distinctive, needle-like foliage and small, yellow flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat various health conditions due to the presence of thevetin, a toxic compound that has been used in traditional medicine to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The health benefits of Podocarpus polystachyus subsp. thevetiifolius include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to exercise caution when using Thevetia Podocarpus due to its toxicity and potential side effects.
